hawkish_phrases: most participants continued to see significant upside risks to inflation, which could require further tightening of monetary policy
The Committee remains highly attentive to inflation risks
further evidence would be required for them to be confident that inflation was clearly on a path toward the Committee's 2 percent objective
determining the extent of additional policy firming that may be appropriate
dovish_phrases: tighter credit conditions for households and businesses were likely to weigh on economic activity, hiring, and inflation
A couple of participants indicated that they favored leaving the target range for the federal funds rate unchanged
some participants commented that even though economic activity had been resilient and the labor market had remained strong, there continued to be downside risks to economic activity
A number of participants judged that, with the stance of monetary policy in restrictive territory, risks to the achievement of the Committee's goals had become more two sided
the possibility that the macroeconomic effects of the tightening in financial conditions since the beginning of last year could prove more substantial than anticipated
summary: The Committee unanimously voted to raise the target range for the federal funds rate by 25bp to 5-1/4 to 5-1/2 percent, with explicit language regarding determining the extent of additional policy firming that may be appropriate and most participants seeing significant upside risks to inflation requiring potential further tightening.
